segments.title: 小兔子大梦想 / Small Bunny, Big Dream
segments.summary: 小兔子朱迪·霍普斯怀揣成为警察的梦想，尽管家人认为兔子不适合从警。童年被狐狸吉登欺负的经历让她更加坚定。在警校中，面对歧视与嘲讽，她凭借毅力成为首位兔子警官，并满怀憧憬前往动物城Zootopia开始她的警察生涯。

segments.title: 偏见与突破 / Bias & Breakthrough
segments.summary: 兔警官朱迪报到后遭遇轻视，被分配停车执勤而非重要案件。她通过高效开罚单证明能力，并帮助被歧视的狐狸尼克为儿子买冰淇淋，践行'在Zootopia，任何人都可以成为任何想成为的人'的信念。

segments.title: 偏见与偏见 / Prejudice & Prejudice
segments.summary: 交通警朱迪试图逮捕街头小贩尼克，却因他的狡辩而失败。两人互相嘲讽对方出身与梦想，朱迪被父母发现自己的工作与理想落差，备受打击。然而，当商店抢劫案发生时，朱迪仍勇敢追捕嫌犯，展现了她不屈的警察本色。

segments.title: 不懈追查 / Relentless Pursuit
segments.summary: 朱迪警员不顾上司反对，接手寻找失踪水獭奥顿顿的案件。面临48小时破案或辞职的威胁，她与狐狸尼克联手调查。尽管线索稀少，朱迪凭借聪明才智和执着精神，一步步揭开案件线索，尽管遭遇树懒车管所的缓慢拖延，她仍坚持追寻真相。

segments.title: 夜嚎之谜 / Night Howler Mystery
segments.summary: 朱迪和尼克通过树懒Flash查询车牌，发现线索指向犯罪老大Big先生。在Big先生的婚礼上，朱迪凭救命之恩获得帮助，获知司机Manchas见过Otterton变野。调查中Manchas突然变得极具攻击性，两人被迫逃亡，留下'夜嚎者'的关键线索。

segments.title: 尼克的故事 / Nick's Story
segments.summary: 朱迪因调查动物'变野蛮'现象被警长刁难并剥夺警徽。尼克挺身相助，讲述童年因狐狸身份而受歧视的经历。两人利用交通摄像头系统发现'夜行者'线索，追踪嫌疑人向城市外逃窜。

segments.title: 偏见之痛 / Pain of Prejudice
segments.summary: 朱迪和尼克发现失踪动物全是捕食者，被秘密关押。在新闻发布会上，朱迪暗示捕食者可能回归'野蛮天性'，深深伤害了尼克。城市种族矛盾激化，尼克被调离前台岗位，朱迪对自己的言论造成严重后果感到愧疚，最终放弃了警徽。

segments.title: 真相与盟友 / Truth and Allies
segments.summary: 朱迪从吉迪恩处得知夜嚎花的真相，意识到食肉动物的疯狂并非本性而是人为。她寻求尼克的帮助，两人重新联手追踪夜嚎花的来源，试图揭露有人故意使用这种毒花使食肉动物发狂的阴谋。

segments.title: 理解与包容 / Understanding & Inclusion
segments.summary: Bellwether阴谋被揭露后，城市开始愈合。朱迪发表演讲，承认Zootopia的现实复杂性，呼吁市民尝试理解彼此。尼克成为首只狐狸警察，与朱迪搭档执行新任务，象征着种群的融合与进步。两个朋友通过幽默互动展现深厚的友谊。

subtitles.text: these were the forces that ruled our world.
A world where prey were scared of predators.
And predators had an uncontrollable, biological urge...
Back then, the world was divided in two.
And moved beyond our primitive, savage ways.
Now, predator and prey live in harmony.
And every young mammal has multitudinous opportunities.
I don't have to cower in a herd anymore.
Instead, I can be an astronaut.
I don't have to be a lonely hunter anymore.
Today, I can hunt for tax exemptions.
And I can make the world a better place.
Bunny cop? That is the most stupidest thing I ever heard.
It may seem impossible to small minds...
I'm looking at you, Gideon Grey.
stands the great city of Zootopia!
Where our ancestors first joined together in peace...
and declared that anyone can be anything!
Judy, you ever wonder how your mom and me got to be so darn happy?
Well, we gave up on our dreams and we settled. Right, Bon?
Oh, yes, that's right, Stu. We settled hard.
See, that's the beauty of complacency, Jude.
If you don't try anything new, you'll never fail.
impossible even, for you to become a police officer.
Right. There's never been a bunny cop. Bunnies don't do that.
Then, I guess I'll have to be the first one.
Because I am gonna make the world a better place.
Or, heck, you know, you want to talk about making the world a better place...
no better way to do it than becoming a carrot farmer.
your 275 brothers and sisters. We're changing the world!
Amen to that. Carrot farming is a noble profession.
Just putting the seeds in the ground.
At one with the soil. Just getting covered in dirt.
You get it, honey. It's great to have dreams.
Yeah. Just as long as you don't believe in them too much.
Give me your tickets right now...
or I'm gonna kick your meek little sheep butt.
What are you gonna do? Cry?
You heard her. Cut it out.
What crazy world are you living in
where you think a bunny could be a cop?
But watch out, because I'm a fox,
and like you said in your dumb little stage play...
us predators used to eat prey.
And that killer instinct is still in our "Dunnah."
I'm pretty much sure it's pronounced "DNA."
Don't tell me what I know, Travis.
- You scared now? - Look at her nose twitch!
You don't know when to quit, do you?
I want you to remember this moment...
the next time you think you will ever be anything more than just a stupid...
Yeah, that Gideon Grey doesn't know what he's talking about.
Well, he was right about one thing.
I don't know when to quit.
Zootopia has 12 unique ecosystems within its city limits.
Rainforest District, to name a few.
You're gonna have to master all of them
before you hit the streets, or guess what?
Filthy toilet! You're dead, Fluff Butt.
Just quit and go home, fuzzy bunny!
There's never been a bunny cop.
Just a stupid, carrot-farming dumb bunny,
As mayor of Zootopia, I am proud to announce...
that my Mammal Inclusion Initiative has produced...
Valedictorian of her class, ZPD's very first rabbit officer...
it is my great privilege to officially assign you...
to the heart of Zootopia: Precinct One.
I won't let you down. This has been my dream since I was a kid.
It's a real proud day for us little guys.
Bellwether, make room, will you? Come on.
Okay, Officer Hopps. Let's see those teeth!
- Officer Hopps, right here! - Look this way please!
We're real proud of you, Judy.
Really, it's kind of a proud-scared combo.
I mean, Zootopia. So far away. Such a big city.
I've been working for this my whole life.
We know. And we're just a little excited for you, but terrified.
The only thing we have to fear is fear itself.
And also bears. We have bears to fear, too.
Say nothing of lions and wolves.
You play cribbage with a weasel.
And he cheats like there's no tomorrow.
You know what, pretty much all predators. And Zootopia's full of them.
Actually, your father does have a point there. It's in their biology.
Remember what happened with Gideon Grey?
Gideon Grey was a jerk who happened to be a fox.
I know plenty of bunnies who are jerks.
Sure, we all do. Absolutely. But just in case...
we made you a little care package to take with you.
- And I put some snacks in there. - This is fox deterrent.
Yeah, that's safe, to have that.
The deterrent and the repellent, that's all she needs.
- Check this out! - For goodness sake.
She has no need for a fox Taser, Stu.
Come on. When is there not a need for a fox Taser?
Okay, look! I will take this, to make you stop talking.
Oh, cripes, here come the waterworks.
I messed up tonight I lost another fight
Lost to myself But I'll just start again
I keep on hitting the ground
But I always get up now
They fall down and get up
Nobody learns without getting it wrong
I won't give up No I won't give in
Till I reach the end And then I'll start again
I want to try everything I want to try even though I could fail
I won't give up No, I won't give in
No, I won't leave I want to try everything
I want to try even though I could fail
I'll keep on making those new mistakes
And I'll keep on making them every day
Welcome to the Grand Pangolin Arms.
Complimentary delousing once a month. Don't lose your key.
Hi! I'm Judy, your new neighbor.
Don't expect us to apologize for it.
- You shut up! - You shut up!
They really did hire a bunny! What!
I gotta tell you, you are even cuter than I thought you'd be!
You probably didn't know, but a bunny can call another bunny "cute"...
but when other animals do it...
I am so sorry! Me, Benjamin Clawhauser...
the guy everyone thinks is just a flabby, donut-loving cop, stereotyping you.
No, it's okay. Oh, you've actually got... There's a...
There you went, you little dickens!
I should get to roll call, which way do I...?
Bullpen's over there to the left.
That poor little bunny's gonna get eaten alive.
You ready to make the world a better place?
I've got three items on the docket.
First... we need to acknowledge the elephant in the room.
There are some new recruits with us I should introduce...
Finally, we have 14 missing mammal cases.
All predators, from a giant polar bear to a teensy little otter.
And City Hall is right up my tail to find them.
Your teams take missing mammals from the Rainforest District.
Sir, you said there were 14 missing mammal cases.
- So? - So, I can handle one.
You probably forgot, but I was top of my class at the Academy.
Sir, I'm not just some token bunny.
Well, then writing 100 tickets a day should be easy.
100 tickets. I'm not gonna write 100 tickets.
Hey, watch where you're going, fox!
I don't know what you're doing skulking around during daylight hours...
but I don't want any trouble in here. So, hit the road.
I'm not looking for any trouble either, sir.
I simply want to buy a Jumbo Pop...
You want the red or the blue, pal?
Listen, buddy. What? There aren't any fox ice cream joints...
There are. It's just, my boy, this goofy little stinker...
he loves all things elephant. Wants to be one when he grows up.
Who the heck am I to crush his little dreams? Right?
You probably can't read, fox, but the sign says...
"We reserve the right to refuse service...
You're gonna have to wait your turn like everyone else, meter maid.
Are your customers aware they're getting snot and mucus
I don't want to cause you any trouble, but I believe scooping ice cream...
with an ungloved trunk is a Class 3 health code violation.
Which is kind of a big deal.
Of course, I could let you off with a warning...
if you were to glove those trunks and, I don't know...
finish selling this nice dad and his son a...
Thank you so much. Thank you.
Oh, no. Are you kidding me? I don't have my wallet.
I'd lose my head if it weren't attached to my neck. That's the truth.
Oh, boy. I'm sorry, pal. Got to be about the worst birthday ever.
Please don't be mad at me.
Officer, I can't thank you enough.
So kind, really. Can I pay you back?
Oh, no, My treat. It just...
You know, it burns me up to see folks
with such backward attitudes toward foxes.
I just wanna say you're a great dad and just a...
Well, that is high praise. It's rare that I find someone so non-patronizing.
You want to be an elephant when you grow up? You be an elephant.
Boy, I tell him that all the time.
All right, here you go. Two paws. Yeah.
Look at that smile. That's a "happy birthday" smile.
All right, give her a little bye-bye toot-toot.
Way to work that diaper, big guy.
Hey, no kiss bye-bye for daddy?
You kiss me tomorrow, I'll bite your face off.
Well. I stood up for you, and you lied to me.
And I'm not the liar. He is.
All right, slick Nick, you're under arrest.
Gee, I don't know. How about selling food without a permit.
Transporting undeclared commerce across borough lines.
And I didn't falsely advertise anything. Take care.
You told that mouse the popsicle sticks were redwood!
That's right. "Red wood." With a space in the middle.
You can't touch me, Carrots. I've been doing this since I was born.
You're gonna want to refrain from calling me Carrots.
My bad. I just naturally assumed
you came from some little carrot-choked Podunk, no?
Podunk is in Deerbrooke County, and I grew up in Bunnyburrow.
Okay. Tell me if this story sounds familiar.
Naive little hick with good grades and big ideas...
decides, "Hey, look at me! I'm gonna move to Zootopia...
where predators and prey live in harmony and sing "Kumbaya."
we don't all get along. And that dream of becoming a big city cop?
Double whoopsie. She's a meter maid. And, whoopsie number three-sie...
no one cares about her or her dreams.
And soon enough, those dreams die
and our bunny sinks into emotional and literal squalor...
living in a box under a bridge...
till finally she has no choice but to go back home...
with that cute, fuzzy-wuzzy little tail between her legs...
You're from Bunnyburrow, is that what you said?
So how about a carrot farmer. That sound about right?
Be careful, now, or it won't just be your dreams getting crushed.
Hey! No one tells me what I can or can't be!
who never had the guts to try to be anything more than a popsicle hustler.
All right, look. Everyone comes to Zootopia...
thinking they can be anything they want.
You can only be what you are.
Right. And that's not wet cement.
You'll never be a real cop.
You're a cute meter maid, though.
You can't do nothing right, babe
Hey there, Jude the dude. How was your first day on the force?
And more. Everyone's so nice and...
- I feel like I'm making a difference. - Wait a second.
Holy cripes, Bonnie, look at that.
Judy, are you a meter maid?
This? No! Oh, no. This is just a temporary thing.
It's the safest job on the force!
She's not a real cop. Our prayers have been answered!
- Glorious day! - Meter maid! Meter maid!
- Meter maid! Meter maid! - Dad. Dad. Dad!
It's been a really long day. I should really...
That's right, you get some rest.
Those meters aren't gonna maid themselves.
Hey, buddy, turn down that depressing music.
Leave the meter maid alone. Didn't you hear her conversation?
- Oh, shut up! - You shut up!
- You shut up! - You shut up!
Yeah, but it might be worse!
My mommy says she wishes you were dead.
Uncool, rabbit. My tax dollars pay your salary.
I am a real cop. I am a real cop. I am a real cop.
Sir. If you have a grievance, you may contest your citation in traffic court.
What are you talking about? My shop!
Are you a cop or not?
Yes! Don't worry, sir! I've got this!
Stop! Stop, in the name of the law!
Catch me if you can, Cottontail!
This is Officer McHorn, we got a 10-31.
Officer Hopps. I am in pursuit!
Hey, meter maid! Wait for the real cops!
Oh, my God. Did you see those leopard print jeggings?
You're gonna have to be patient and wait in line just like everyone else...
you did stop a master criminal from stealing two dozen moldy onions.
Hate to disagree with you, sir, but those aren't onions.
Those are a crocus varietal called midnicampum holicithias.
I grew up in a family where plant husbandry was kind of a thing.
Sir, I got the bad guy.
Your job is putting tickets on parked cars!
Chief, Mrs. Otterton's here to see you again.
Okay, I just didn't know if you'd want to take it this time.
I don't want to be a meter maid, I want to be a real cop.
Do you think the mayor asked me what I wanted when he assigned you to me?
Life isn't some cartoon musical where you sing a little song...
and your insipid dreams magically come true.
Chief Bogo, please. Five minutes of your time.
I tried to stop her. She is super-slippery.
Ma'am, as I've told you, we're doing everything we can.
My husband has been missing for 10 days. His name is Emmitt Otterton.
We have two beautiful children. He would never just disappear.
Ma'am, our detectives are very busy.
Please. There's got to be someone to find my Emmitt.
Bless you. Bless you, little bunny.
Find my Emmitt. Bring him home...
to me and my babies, please.
Mrs. Otterton, please wait out here.
Of course. Thank you both so much.
Now. I'm going to open this door...
and you're going to tell that otter you're a former meter maid...
who will not be taking the case!
I just heard Officer Hopps is taking the case.
The Mammal Inclusion Initiative is really starting to pay off.
Mayor Lionheart is just gonna be so jazzed!
No, let's not tell the Mayor just yet.
And I've sent it and it is done, so I did do that.
Well, I'd say the case is in good hands.
Us little guys really need to stick together, right?
Just call me if you ever need anything, okay?
You've always got a friend at City Hall, Judy.
That's two days to find Emmitt Otterton.
But, you strike out, you resign.
Splendid. Clawhauser will give you the complete case file.
Yikes! That is the smallest case file I've ever seen.
And you're not in the computer system yet, so resources, none!
I hope you didn't stake your career on cracking this one!
Can I just borrow... Thank you.
Yeah. Because that... What does that mean?
and I'm here to ask you some questions about a case.
What happened, meter maid? Did someone steal a traffic cone?
Hey, Carrots, you're going to wake the baby. I gotta get to work.
I think your $10 worth of popsicles can wait.
I make 200 bucks a day, Fluff!
365 days a year, since I was 12.
And time is money. Hop along.
Please, just look at the picture.
You sold Mr. Otterton that popsicle, right? Do you know him?
And I also know that, somewhere...
there's a toy store missing its stuffed animal,
so why don't you get back to your box?
Fine. Then we'll have to do this the hard way.
Did you just boot my stroller?
Nicholas Wilde, you are under arrest.
Yeah, $200 a day, 365 days a year, since you were 12.
That's two decades, so times 20, which is $1,460,000, I think.
I mean, I am just a dumb bunny, but we are good at multiplying.
Anyway, according to your tax forms...
you reported, let me see here... zero!
Unfortunately, lying on a federal form is a punishable offense.
Well, it's my word against yours.
365 days a year, since I was 12.
Actually, it's your word against yours.
And if you want this pen, you're going to help me find this poor, missing otter...
or the only place you'll be selling popsicles is the prison cafeteria.
You a cop now, Nick. You gonna need one of these.
I don't know where he is. I only saw where he went.
It's not exactly a place for...
Don't call me cute. Get in the car.
You know, I'm going to hit the pause button right there...
because we're all good on Bunny Scout cookies.
I'm looking for a missing mammal, Emmitt Otterton, right here...
who may have frequented this establishment.
Haven't seen him in a couple of weeks.
But, hey, you should talk to his yoga instructor.
I'd be happy to take you back.
I'd appreciate that more than you can imagine, it would be such...
For sure, we're a naturalist club.
Yeah. In Zootopia, anyone can be anything.
Nangi's just on the other side of the Pleasure Pool.
Because if so, there is no shame in calling it quits.
Yeah, some mammals say the naturalist life is weird...
but you know what I say is weird?
As you can see, Nangi's an elephant, so she'll totally remember everything.
These dudes have some questions about Emmitt the otter.
Emmitt Otterton? Been coming to your yoga class for like...
I have no memory of this beaver.
He was here a couple Wednesdays ago, remember?
Yeah, he was wearing a green cable-knit sweater vest...
and a new pair of corduroy slacks. And a paisley tie, sweet Windsor knot.
And we both walked him out, and he got into this big old white car
Needed a tune-up. The third cylinder wasn't firing.
You didn't happen to catch the license plate number, did you?
This is a lot of great info. Thank you.
Told you Nangi has a mind like a steel trap.
I wish I had a memory like an elephant.
You are welcome for the clue...
and seeing as how any moron can run a plate,
I will take that pen and bid you adieu.
The plate. I can't run a plate.
I'm not in the system yet.
Gosh. If only there were a moron around who were up to the task.
Rabbit, I did what you asked! You can't keep me on the hook forever.
Not forever. Well, I only have 36 hours left to solve this case.
So can you run the plate or not?
I have a pal at the DMV
Flash is the fastest guy in there.
If you need something done, he's on it.
I hope so. We are really fighting the clock and every minute counts.
You said this was going to be quick!
Are you saying that because he's a sloth, he can't be fast?
I thought in Zootopia, anyone could be anything.
Flash, Flash, hundred-yard dash. Buddy, it's nice to see you.
Hey, Flash, I'd love you to meet my friend.
Officer Judy Hopps, ZPD, how are you?
- do... - I was hoping you could run a...
Well, I was hoping you could...
Well, I was hoping you could run a plate for us.
We are in a really big hurry.
Hey, Flash, want to hear a joke?
Okay. What do you call a three-humped camel?
Can we please just focus on the...
A three-humped camel? "Pregnant!" Okay, great, we got it.
It's registered to Tundratown Limo Service.
A limo took Otterton, and the limo's in Tundratown!
Way to hustle, bud. I love you. I owe you.
Hurry! We gotta beat the rush hour, and...
And I will bet you you don't have a warrant to get in.
You wasted the day on purpose.
Madam, I have a fake badge.
I would never impede your pretend investigation.
It's not a pretend investigation. Look, see? See him? This otter is missing.
Then they should've gotten a real cop to find him.
Does seeing me fail somehow make you feel better
about your own sad, miserable life?
Fine. We are done. Here's your pen.
First off, you throw like a bunny.
Second, you're a very sore loser. See you later, Officer Fluff.
So sad this is over. I wish I could've helped more.
The thing is, you don't need a warrant if you have probable cause...
and I'm pretty sure I saw a shifty lowlife climbing the fence.
So you're helping plenty. Come on.
The Velvety Pipes of Jerry Vole.
Carrots, if your otter was here, he had a very bad day.
You ever seen anything like this?
Polar bear fur, Rat Pack music, fancy cup?
I know whose car this is. We got to go.
The most feared crime-boss in Tundratown. They call him Mr. Big.
And he does not like me. So we gotta go!
I'm not leaving. This is a crime scene.
It's gonna be an even bigger crime scene if Mr. Big finds me here...
And is that Kevin? Long time, no see.
And, speaking of "no see," how about you forget you saw me?
What did you do that made Mr. Big so mad at you?
I may have sold him a very expensive wool rug...
that was made from the fur of a skunk...
Stop talking, stop talking, stop talking!
Mr. Big, sir. This is a simple...
You come here, unannounced, on the day my daughter is to be married.
Well, actually, we were brought here against our will, so...
The point is, I did not know that it was your car...
and I certainly did not know about your daughter's wedding.
I welcomed you into my home.
And how did you repay my generosity?
With a rug made from the butt of a skunk.
You disrespected my Gram-mama, who I buried in that skunk butt rug.
I told you never to show your face here again but here you are...
This... mime cannot speak. You can't speak if you're a mime.
And I'm on the Emmitt Otterton case.
And my evidence puts him in your car.
So intimidate me all you want.
I'm going to find out what you did to that otter if it's the last thing I do.
Then I have only one request.
I didn't see nothing! I'm not saying nothing!
If you're mad at me about the rug, I've got more rugs!
Daddy! It's time for our dance!
No icing anyone at my wedding!
I have to, baby. Daddy has to.
She's the bunny that saved my life yesterday!
You've done me a great service.
I will help you find the otter.
I will take your kindness and pay it forward.
He's like a part of the family.
He had something important he wanted to discuss.
That's why I sent that car to pick him up.
Ripped up the car, scared my driver half to death...
But he's a sweet little otter.
My child, we may be evolved, but deep down...
You want to find Otterton, talk to the driver of that car.
Only he can tell you more.
We just wanna know what happened to Emmitt Otterton.
He just kept yelling about the Night Howlers.
Over and over. The Night Howlers.
So you know about the Night Howlers, too?
Because the Night Howlers are exactly what we are here to talk about.
So, you just open the door...
and tell us what you know...
and we will tell you what we know. Okay?
Are you familiar with Gazelle, greatest singer of our lifetime, angel with horns?
Okay, hold on. Keep watching. Who's that beside her? Who is it?
Wow, you are one hot dancer, Benjamin Clawhauser.
It's me! Did you think it was real? It looks so real!
It's not, it's just a new app. Hold on a second.
Listen to me, we have a 10-91! Jaguar gone savage!
Now, I can tell you're a little tense...
so I'm just gonna give you a little personal space!
Rabbit, whatever you do, do not let go!
